page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of dedicated RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. Cache specifications include 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and a 33 MB L3 cache. It does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 268.78 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 131.67 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Passmark memory and database operations benchmarks also demonstrate strong performance, with database operations scoring 22,557.31. Software compilation using Clang on Geekbench is another strong area, registering a score of 40,304.0. Standard CPU tasks, such as stress-ng multi-core and single-core tests, fall within the average range. Large-scale LLM text generation represents a performance bottleneck, yielding a weak result of 2.67 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-64 offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads that require substantial parallel processing without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The instance is well-suited for multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing. However, workloads requiring high-speed local storage or heavy real-time LLM text generation on large models are not recommended due to the lack of local NVMe drives and weak large-model generation benchmarks.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 is an x86_64 general-purpose server configured with 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of dedicated RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz, this instance features a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lacks bundled local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong capabilities in uncached memory bandwidth, software compilation, and database operations, while standard CPU and cryptographic tasks perform in the average tier. Conversely, text generation for large 70B LLM models is a notable bottleneck. Qualitatively, the instance provides a cost-effective, high-density resource profile for parallel processing workloads. It is highly suited for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 is a general-purpose server featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It delivers strong memory bandwidth and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-64 is a general-purpose server with 64 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, offering strong memory bandwidth and database performance.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU general-purpose instance delivering strong memory bandwidth and database performance for parallel enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor
Strong uncached memory bandwidth and database operation performance
Excellent performance for multi-threaded software compilation
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for database hosting, enterprise applications, and web serving
